reforms.description: Germany is planning to introduce an R&D tax credit to support innovative firms, aiming to enhance their capacity for research and development. This initiative will complement existing direct R&D support and aims to improve the availability of risk financing for innovation projects, particularly for small firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the capacity for R&D among firms is expected to lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans for the R&D tax credit.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of an R&D tax credit is a significant reform but not a structural change.
reforms.source_quote: Germany should maintain direct research funding, notably by using public tenders, and consider implementing an R&D tax credit for innovative firms.

reforms.description: Germany intends to improve the availability of risk financing to support innovative young companies, which are currently underrepresented in the country. This includes plans to enhance the framework conditions for venture capital, implementing the EU Directive on Alternative Investment Fund Managers to provide better exit possibilities for venture capitalists.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving access to venture capital is expected to foster the growth of innovative startups, enhancing overall economic dynamism.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced intentions to improve venture capital conditions.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly alters the venture capital landscape, aiming for structural improvements.
reforms.source_quote: Similarly, raising the availability of risk financing is important to foster innovative young companies, which are underrepresented in Germany compared to other innovative countries.

reforms.description: Germany has implemented measures to improve the accessibility of tertiary education, including increased autonomy for universities to select students and introduce tuition fees, along with financial support for low-income students. Additionally, the 'Recognition Act' facilitates the integration of skilled migrants into the labor market by granting them the legal right to receive an official evaluation of their foreign qualifications.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving access to education and integrating skilled migrants enhances the talent pool, which is crucial for innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures have been put into effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms significantly improve human capital but do not represent a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: Germany has implemented measures facilitating access to university for students with low social background, such as the provision of loans to finance tuition fees.

reforms.description: Germany is investing in the expansion and improvement of electricity transmission and distribution networks to accommodate renewable energy sources, which is crucial for integrating decentralized energy production. This investment is part of the 6th Energy Research Programme, which allocates EUR 3.5 billion for energy research from 2011 to 2014, marking a 75% increase compared to previous funding periods.
reforms.component_name: Electricity Network Improvement and Energy Research Funding
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in energy research and infrastructure is expected to enhance innovation in the energy sector and support the transition to renewable energy.
reforms.status_evidence: The investment and funding have been executed.
reforms.importance_rationale: The significant funding and infrastructure improvements represent a major reform in energy research.
reforms.source_quote: The Cabinet recently adopted the 6th Energy Research Programme providing EUR 3.5 billion for energy research on the period 2011-14, a 75% increase compared to 2006-09.
